description: A repo-agnostic autonomous agent harness that triages open source GitHub issues, runs a human-in-the-loop design review via email, and ships verified fixes as PRs from a fork. Agents never write directly to upstream — the only path is a reviewed PR from a fork. Configured per-repo via a manifest. See plan/PRD v0.1 (May 2026).

userStories.title: Repo manifest loader and schema
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a manifest schema and loader so the orchestrator can configure itself per-repo without code changes. Manifest fields: repo, trigger_label (default agent-fix), fork_org, branch_prefix (default agent/scope-), test_command, approval_keywords, pm_email, issue_types, sandbox_services, max_retries (default 3), skip_pm_gate.
acceptanceCriteria: Manifest schema defined (JSON Schema or equivalent) covering every field in PRD section 3.1
Loader reads a YAML or JSON manifest from a configurable path and validates against the schema
Defaults applied when fields are omitted (trigger_label=agent-fix, branch_prefix=agent/scope-, max_retries=3)
Invalid manifests fail fast with a clear, field-level error message
Example manifest from PRD section 3.2 (Arize-ai/openinference) loads and validates successfully
Unit tests cover: valid manifest, missing required field, invalid types, defaults applied
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/manifest/. Schema in schema.ts (JSON Schema draft-07 with ajv), loader in loader.ts (YAML+JSON, defaults, field-level errors), types in types.ts. Example manifest in examples/openinference.yaml. Unit tests in src/manifest/__tests__/loader.test.ts covering valid manifest, missing required fields, invalid types, defaults applied, and file-not-found.

userStories.title: Orchestrator state machine and persistence
userStories.description: As the system, I need a central state machine that owns full pipeline state for an issue group so runs can survive restarts and resume from the last completed stage. States: TRIGGERED, TRIAGING, PM_SCORING, EMAIL_PENDING, SWEEP_PENDING, FORKING, AGENT_RUNNING, SANDBOX_RUNNING, EVAL_RUNNING, PR_OPEN, FAILED, SKIPPED.
acceptanceCriteria: State machine implemented with all 12 states from PRD section 7.2 and explicit allowed transitions
Run state persisted to SQLite (single-machine default) with schema migrations
On restart, in-flight runs resume from their last persisted state
Illegal transitions raise an error and do not mutate state
Each state transition is logged with timestamp, run_id, and prior/new state
Unit tests cover happy path, restart-resume, and illegal transition rejection
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/orchestrator/. Types in types.ts (RunState enum with 12 states, Run/TransitionLogEntry interfaces, IllegalTransitionError, terminal/in-flight helpers). Transitions map in transitions.ts with explicit allowed transitions per state. Persistence in persistence.ts (better-sqlite3, WAL mode, FK constraints, CHECK constraints on state, schema migrations table, atomic transitions via SQLite transactions, retry_count column). StateMachine class in state-machine.ts (createRun, transition with validation, getRun, getRunsByState, getInFlightRuns for restart-resume, getTransitionLog). Unit tests in __tests__/state-machine.test.ts covering: happy path (full pipeline, email loop, docs fast path, skip to SKIPPED, FAILED from all states), retry path (EVAL_RUNNING→AGENT_RUNNING increments retry_count), illegal transitions (terminal states, invalid forward, no log entry on rejection, non-existent run), transition logging, restart-resume (file-backed DB, new instance discovers in-flight runs, continues transitions), schema migration idempotency.

userStories.title: GitHub webhook listener and event router
userStories.description: As the system, I need to receive GitHub issue.opened and issue.labeled events and dispatch them into the orchestrator after looking up the matching repo manifest.
acceptanceCriteria: HTTP endpoint accepts GitHub webhook POSTs and verifies the X-Hub-Signature-256 HMAC
Handles issue.opened and issue.labeled events; ignores all other event types
Looks up the manifest for the source repo; events for repos with no manifest enter SKIPPED state
Events without the trigger_label (or skip_pm_gate label) are ignored on issue.labeled
Creates a new orchestrator run in TRIGGERED state and returns 202 Accepted within 1 second
Webhook secret is read from environment, never logged
Integration test sends a signed payload and verifies a run is created
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/webhook/. Types in types.ts (IssueEvent, GitHubIssue, WebhookResult). Signature verification in signature.ts (HMAC SHA-256 with constant-time comparison via crypto.timingSafeEqual). Event router in router.ts (ManifestRegistry interface, routeEvent function handling issue.opened and issue.labeled, trigger_label/skip_pm_gate matching, SKIPPED for repos without manifest). HTTP server in server.ts (Node http module, POST /webhook endpoint, signature verification, 202 Accepted for processed events, 200 for ignored). Integration tests in __tests__/webhook.test.ts covering: valid/invalid/missing signature, non-issue events ignored, issue.opened creates TRIGGERED run, no-manifest repos enter SKIPPED, trigger_label matching, non-matching labels ignored, skip_pm_gate label support, response time under 1 second, secret never in response.

userStories.title: Triage agent
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need a triage agent that classifies an issue (bug_fix / new_feature / docs), identifies the affected module, and emits a confidence score and one-sentence summary so downstream agents have structured context.
acceptanceCriteria: Inputs: issue title, body, labels, author; manifest module taxonomy; shallow repo tree (top two directory levels)
Outputs: issue_type in {bug_fix, new_feature, docs}, affected_module path, confidence float 0-1, summary string
Confidence below 0.6 posts a clarification comment on the issue and halts the run pending a reply
Routes bug_fix and new_feature to PM agent; routes docs to docs agent (fast path); routes skip_pm_gate label directly to fork creation
Triage completes in under 90 seconds for a typical issue
Unit tests with fixture issues cover each issue_type, low-confidence path, and skip_pm_gate path
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/triage-types.ts, src/triage-agent.ts, src/triage-index.ts. Types: TriageInput (title, body, labels, author, moduleTaxonomy, repoTree, hasSkipPmGate), TriageResult (issueType, affectedModule, confidence, summary), TriageRouting union type, TriageClassifier/IssueCommenter interfaces. HeuristicClassifier: label-based then keyword-based classification, path-matching module identification, multi-factor confidence scoring. triageIssue: routes bug_fix/new_feature to PM agent, docs to docs agent (fast path), skip_pm_gate to fork creation; confidence < 0.6 posts clarification comment and halts. runTriage: full pipeline with side effects (comment posting). Unit tests in src/triage-agent.test.ts: 25+ test cases covering each issue_type from labels and keywords, affected module identification, confidence scoring (high/low/conflicting), summary generation, routing (PM/docs/fork/clarify), low-confidence comment posting, skip_pm_gate path, timing under 90s.

userStories.title: PM agent design scoring (no email yet)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator (Phase 1), I need a PM agent that scores design complexity and auto-routes based on the score, so the core pipeline can run end-to-end before the email loop is built.
acceptanceCriteria: Searches all open issues for the same module / error pattern / API surface
Reads the last 30 merged PRs touching the affected module
Reads relevant spec or design docs in the repo
Emits design_needed boolean and a reasoning summary

Heuristics from PRD section 4.2: new/changed public API, >=3 related open issues, intentional-but-contested behaviour, change spans >2 modules, or title/body contains 'rethink', 'redesign', 'breaking', 'architecture' all set design_needed=true
In Phase 1 mode, design_needed=true auto-routes to FAILED with a 'design needed - email loop not yet implemented' note; design_needed=false routes to FORKING
Unit tests cover each scoring heuristic
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/pm-agent-types.ts, src/pm-agent.ts, src/pm-agent-index.ts. Types: PMScoringInput (issueType, affectedModule, summary, title, body, labels, relatedIssues, recentPRs, designDocs), PMScoringResult (designNeeded, reasoning, signals), PMRouting union type (route_forking | route_failed), DesignSignal interface, plus IssueSearcher/PRFetcher/DesignDocFinder interfaces for testability. Heuristics implemented: design_keywords (rethink/redesign/breaking/architecture in title/body), related_issues_count (>=3 triggers), public_api_change (API keywords + API surface files in recent PRs), contested_behaviour (intentional/wontfix/controversial keywords + labels), multi_module_span (>2 top-level modules from PRs/body). scoreDesign returns all 5 signals with triggered/detail. routePMResult: designNeeded=true routes to FAILED with 'design needed - email loop not yet implemented' note; designNeeded=false routes to FORKING. runPMScoring: full pipeline function. Unit tests in src/pm-agent.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering each heuristic individually (design keywords case-insensitive, related issues threshold boundary, public API from keywords and PR files, contested behaviour from text and labels, multi-module from PRs and body paths), integration tests for scoreDesign (no triggers, single trigger, multiple triggers, reasoning content), routePMResult (both paths), and runPMScoring full pipeline (simple bug to FORKING, design-needed to FAILED, many related issues to FAILED, result structure).

userStories.title: Fork creation and branch management
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need to fork the upstream repo into fork_org and create a deterministically named branch per issue group, so all agent writes are isolated from upstream.
acceptanceCriteria: One fork per confirmed issue group (not per issue), created under fork_org via the GitHub API
If a fork already exists for fork_org/repo, sync it with upstream main before branching
Branch name format: {branch_prefix}{issue-ids-sorted-numerically}, e.g. agent/scope-142-156-203
If the branch already exists, reset it to fork main (treated as a retry)
No agent ever receives a token with write access to upstream
Integration test against a sandbox upstream repo creates a fork, branches deterministically, and reuses the existing fork on a second run
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/fork-types.ts, src/fork-manager.ts, src/fork-index.ts. Types: ForkConfig (upstream, forkOrg, branchPrefix, issueIds), ForkResult (forkFullName, forkCreated, forkSynced, branchName, branchReset), GitHubClient interface (repoExists, createFork, syncFork, getDefaultBranch, getBranchSha, createBranch, updateBranchRef, getTokenScopes), ForkCreationError, BranchCreationError, UpstreamWriteGuardError. Functions: generateBranchName (deterministic, sorted numerically), deriveForkName (owner/repo → forkOrg/repo), verifyNoUpstreamWriteAccess (token scope check), createForkAndBranch (full pipeline: validate → check token → create/reuse fork → sync → create/reset branch). Branch name format: {branch_prefix}{sorted-issue-ids-joined-by-dash}. If branch exists, resets to fork main (retry). All writes go to fork only, never upstream. Unit tests in src/fork-manager.test.ts: 25+ test cases covering generateBranchName (sorted, single, custom prefix, numeric sort, immutability), deriveForkName (valid, invalid), createForkAndBranch (new fork creation, fork reuse, sync before branching, fork creation error, sync failure on existing fork, sync tolerance on new fork, deterministic branch name, branch reset on retry, branch creation error, reset error, missing default branch SHA, validation errors for upstream/forkOrg/issueIds, token scope verification, writes only to fork, full integration flow for new fork and retry scenario, second run reuses fork and resets branch, one fork per issue group determinism).

userStories.title: Fix agent operating on the fork branch
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need a fix agent that patches existing code on the fork branch given the agreed design and confirmed issue list as context.
acceptanceCriteria: Receives: agreed design summary, confirmed issue list, affected module source, existing tests for the module, last 20 commits for the module
Reads the full module before making any changes
Makes only targeted changes within the issue scope; does not refactor unrelated code
Writes or updates tests for every code change
Commits with message format: fix({module}): {one-line summary} — closes #{issue_ids}
All writes go to the fork branch only; verified by the GitHub token scope used
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/fix-agent-types.ts, src/fix-agent.ts, src/fix-agent-index.ts. Types: FixAgentInput (designSummary, confirmedIssues, affectedModule, moduleSource, moduleTests, recentCommits, forkFullName, branchName), FixAgentResult (success, changes, testChanges, commitMessage, summary, closesIssues), FileChange (path, action, content), ConfirmedIssue, ModuleCommit, ModuleFile interfaces, FixGenerator/ForkCommitter/RepoFileReader interfaces for testability, FixAgentError, UpstreamWriteAttemptError. Agent pipeline (runFixAgent): verifies fork-only token access → reads full module → generates fix via FixGenerator → validates change scope (only affected module + tests) → validates test coverage (every source change needs tests) → formats commit message fix({module}): {summary} — closes #{ids} → commits to fork branch. Helper functions: formatCommitMessage, extractModuleName (strips src/lib prefix), validateChangeScope, verifyForkOnlyAccess (rejects public_repo/repo scopes), validateTestCoverage, readFullModule. Unit tests in src/fix-agent.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering formatCommitMessage (single/multiple IDs, src/lib prefix stripping), extractModuleName, validateChangeScope (in-module, test files, out-of-scope, empty), validateTestCoverage (covered, uncovered, empty), verifyForkOnlyAccess (narrow scopes pass, broad scopes throw), readFullModule (lists and reads files, empty module), runFixAgent integration (success flow, reads module before generating, commits to fork only, rejects upstream write access, rejects out-of-scope changes, rejects missing tests, handles empty generator output, passes full input to generator, multiple issue IDs, design summary context, new test file creation, commit message format validation).

userStories.title: Basic sandbox runner (test suite only)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator (Phase 1), I need a sandbox runner that executes the manifest test_command on the fork branch via GitHub Actions and captures stdout/stderr/exit code, so fixes are verified before a PR is opened.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggered via workflow_dispatch on the fork using the GitHub API
Runs test_command from the manifest; captures stdout, stderr, and exit code
Sandbox has no external network access except sandbox_services declared in the manifest
Each run is fully isolated; fork branch is checked out fresh; no state persists between runs
Sandbox run duration capped at 15 minutes (configurable per manifest)
Outputs are made available to the eval agent as structured artifacts
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/sandbox-types.ts, src/sandbox-runner.ts, src/sandbox-index.ts. Types: SandboxConfig (forkFullName, branchName, testCommand, sandboxServices, timeoutMinutes), SandboxResult (completed, exitCode, stdout, stderr, durationSeconds, workflowRunUrl, timedOut, workflowRunId), SandboxArtifact (config, result, startedAt, completedAt), ActionsClient interface (triggerWorkflowDispatch, getWorkflowRun, waitForWorkflowRun, getWorkflowRunLogs, uploadArtifact), WorkflowRun, WorkflowRunStatus, WorkflowRunLogs interfaces, SandboxRunError (with phase), SandboxTimeoutError. Constants: SANDBOX_WORKFLOW_FILE='sandbox-test.yml', DEFAULT_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=15, DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=10000. Functions: validateSandboxConfig (validates all fields), buildWorkflowInputs (test_command, branch, timeout, services, network_policy), createSandboxConfig (factory with defaults), buildSandboxArtifact (structured output for eval agent), runSandbox (full pipeline: validate → trigger workflow_dispatch → poll for run → wait for completion/timeout → retrieve logs → build artifact → upload). Network isolation via network_policy input (none when no services, allow:services when declared). Each run is isolated (fresh checkout, no state). Duration capped at configurable timeoutMinutes (default 15). Unit tests in src/sandbox-runner.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering validateSandboxConfig (valid, empty forkFullName, no slash, empty branch, whitespace branch, empty testCommand, zero/negative timeout, error phase), buildWorkflowInputs (test_command, branch, timeout, network_policy none/allow, services join), createSandboxConfig (all fields, default timeout, array copy), buildSandboxArtifact (complete structure), runSandbox integration (dispatch params, poll, wait timeout, logs retrieval, structured artifact, timeout handling, dispatch failure, run not appearing, log failure, artifact upload, non-fatal upload failure, validation before trigger, default/configurable timeout, duration calculation, non-zero exit code, run isolation, sandbox_services in inputs, network_policy, branch checkout).

userStories.title: Eval agent (pass/fail) and PR creation
userStories.description: As the orchestrator (Phase 1), I need an eval agent that produces a pass/fail verdict from sandbox outputs and, on pass, opens a PR from the fork to upstream so a human reviewer can merge.
acceptanceCriteria: Eval agent emits: overall_pass, per_issue_verdict map, regression_detected (false in Phase 1), retry_context (if failing), pr_summary
On overall_pass=true, opens a PR from fork branch to upstream default branch
PR title format: [agent-fix] {one-line summary from eval agent}
PR body includes: agreed design summary, grouped issues with per-issue verdict, link to sandbox run logs
Labels applied: agent-fix plus one label per issue type addressed; reviewers are NOT auto-assigned
On overall_pass=false, run transitions to AGENT_RUNNING for retry up to max_retries (no eval context injection yet — that lands in Phase 3)
After max_retries exceeded, transitions to FAILED
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/eval-agent-types.ts, src/eval-agent.ts, src/eval-agent-index.ts. Types: EvalAgentInput (sandboxArtifact, confirmedIssues, fixSummary, designSummary, forkFullName, branchName, upstreamRepo, upstreamDefaultBranch, issueTypes, retryCount, maxRetries), EvalAgentResult (overallPass, perIssueVerdicts, regressionDetected always false in Phase 1, retryContext, prSummary), EvalRouting union type (open_pr | retry | failed), PRDetails (title, body, labels, head, base), PRClient interface (createPullRequest, addLabels), IssueVerdict (issueNumber, passed, reason), EvalAgentError. Functions: evaluateSandboxResults (pass when completed+exitCode 0+not timed out, per-issue verdicts, retry context with stderr/stdout/command, prSummary), buildPRDetails (title [agent-fix] format, body with design summary + per-issue verdicts + sandbox link + retry info, labels agent-fix + issue types deduplicated, head forkOrg:branch, base upstream default, no reviewers), routeEvalResult (pass→open_pr, fail+retries remaining→retry with context, fail+max retries→failed), runEvalAgent (full pipeline: evaluate → route → create PR on pass with labels, non-fatal label failure). Unit tests in src/eval-agent.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering evaluateSandboxResults (pass on exit 0, fail on non-zero/timeout/incomplete, regressionDetected always false, per-issue verdicts count and pass/fail, stderr in failure reason, retry context with command/exit/stderr/stdout, prSummary content), buildPRDetails (title format, body sections, sandbox link, retry info presence/absence, labels with dedup, head format, base branch, no reviewers), routeEvalResult (pass→open_pr, fail with retries→retry, fail at max→failed, retry context, max retries=0), runEvalAgent integration (PR creation on pass, correct upstream repo, title format, labels added, no PR on failure, retry routing, failed after max retries, EvalAgentError on PR failure, non-fatal label failure, PR body content, head/base format, no reviewers).

userStories.title: Docs agent (fast path)
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need a docs agent that handles issues classified as docs without going through the PM gate, so trivial documentation fixes ship quickly.
acceptanceCriteria: Triggered directly from triage when issue_type=docs
Updates documentation, READMEs, and specs only — never application code
Commits with message format: docs: {one-line summary} — closes #{issue_ids}
Routes to the same sandbox + eval + PR flow as the fix agent
Skips PM agent entirely
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/docs-agent-types.ts, src/docs-agent.ts, src/docs-agent-index.ts. Types: DocsAgentInput (confirmedIssues, affectedModule, docFiles, recentCommits, forkFullName, branchName, triageSummary), DocsAgentResult (success, changes, commitMessage, summary, closesIssues), DocsGenerator/DocsGeneratorOutput interfaces for testability, DocsAgentError (with phase), DOC_FILE_PATTERNS, APP_CODE_EXTENSIONS. Functions: formatDocsCommitMessage (docs: {summary} — closes #{ids}), isDocumentationFile (recognizes .md/.rst/.txt/.adoc/.mdx, docs/ directories, named files like README/CHANGELOG/CONTRIBUTING), isApplicationCode (detects .ts/.js/.py/.go/.rs/.java etc.), validateDocsOnly (rejects any non-documentation file changes), verifyForkOnlyAccess (rejects broad token scopes), readDocFiles (reads existing docs for context), runDocsAgent (full pipeline: verify token → read docs → generate → validate docs-only → format commit → commit to fork). Triggered directly from triage route_docs action. Skips PM agent entirely (no designSummary required). Routes to same sandbox + eval + PR flow (result shape compatible with eval agent). Unit tests in src/docs-agent.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering formatDocsCommitMessage (single/multiple IDs, no module name), isDocumentationFile (.md/.rst/.txt/.adoc/.mdx, docs/ directory, named files, rejects code), isApplicationCode (TS/JS/Python/Go/Rust, no false positives on docs), validateDocsOnly (valid docs, rejects code, rejects unknown, empty, reports all invalid), verifyForkOnlyAccess (narrow pass, empty pass, public_repo/repo throw), readDocFiles (list+read, empty), runDocsAgent integration (success flow, commit message format, multiple IDs, fork-only commit, rejects upstream access, rejects code changes, error includes filenames, no changes returns failure, no commit on empty, reads before generating, passes input to generator, allows docs/ non-md files, allows CHANGELOG/CONTRIBUTING, skips PM, error phases, result compatible with sandbox+eval flow).

userStories.title: Gmail MCP integration (send + watcher)
userStories.description: As the PM agent (Phase 2), I need to send and receive email through the Gmail MCP so design conversations can run end-to-end through the user's existing inbox.
acceptanceCriteria: Send: PM agent posts emails via Gmail MCP with subject [agent-fix] {repo}/{issue_number}: {issue_title}
Reply-to header points to a monitored address that routes back to the orchestrator
Watcher polls Gmail MCP every 60 seconds for unread replies on threads with the [agent-fix] subject prefix
On reply detected: extracts body, appends to conversation history, wakes the PM agent for that run
Approval keyword detection: case-insensitive substring match against manifest approval_keywords
Threading: same subject and thread ID maintained across the full conversation for one issue group
Unit tests cover send, poll, approval detection (positive + negative)
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/gmail-types.ts, src/gmail-mcp.ts, src/gmail-index.ts. Types: GmailMessage (to, subject, body, replyTo, threadId), GmailSendResult (success, messageId, threadId), GmailReply (messageId, threadId, body, from, receivedAt, subject), GmailWatcherConfig (pollIntervalMs, subjectPrefix, monitoredAddress), EmailThread (runId, threadId, subject, conversationHistory), ConversationEntry (role, body, timestamp, messageId), ApprovalDetectionResult (approved, matchedKeyword, replyBody), GmailClient interface (sendEmail, listUnreadMessages, markAsRead), ReplyHandler interface, GmailSendError, GmailWatcherError, SUBJECT_PREFIX='[agent-fix]', DEFAULT_POLL_INTERVAL_MS=60000. Functions: formatSubject ([agent-fix] {repo}/#{issue_number}: {title}), buildEmailMessage (constructs GmailMessage with replyTo pointing to monitored address), sendEmail (sends via GmailClient, throws GmailSendError on failure), detectApproval (case-insensitive substring match against approval_keywords), createEmailThread, appendReplyToThread, appendAgentMessageToThread, buildWatcherQuery (is:unread subject prefix filter). GmailWatcher class: registerThread/unregisterThread, getThread/getThreadByRunId/getRegisteredThreads, start/stop (interval-based polling), poll (queries Gmail MCP for unread replies, matches to registered threads, appends to history, marks as read, calls ReplyHandler.onReply to wake PM agent). sendAndTrack: full send-and-register flow for new or existing threads. Unit tests in src/gmail-mcp.test.ts: 40+ test cases covering formatSubject (prefix, special chars), buildEmailMessage (all fields, threadId, replyTo), sendEmail (success, GmailSendError on failure, error fields), detectApproval (each keyword, case-insensitive, multi-word, no match, empty body, empty keywords, substring match, first match wins, includes body in result), createEmailThread (structure), appendReplyToThread (appends user, immutability), appendAgentMessageToThread, buildWatcherQuery (unread, prefix, address), GmailWatcher (register/unregister, findByRunId, listAll, start/stop/idempotent, poll queries Gmail, correct query, matches registered threads, ignores unregistered, appends to history, marks as read, multiple replies, GmailWatcherError on poll failure, tolerates markAsRead failure), sendAndTrack (new thread, existing thread append, subject/threadId maintained, propagates errors), constants, threading integrity (full conversation flow, correct run ID routing).

userStories.title: PM agent email conversation loop
userStories.description: As the user, I want a multi-turn email design conversation with the PM agent until I send an approval keyword, so I can shape the design before any code is written.
acceptanceCriteria: Initial design brief sent within 5 minutes of a design_needed=true score
Brief contains all 6 fields from PRD section 5.2: issue summary, affected module, related open issues, recent PR context, 2-3 proposed approach options with tradeoffs, open questions
On each user reply, PM agent re-reads full thread history and responds within 60 seconds
PM agent never restates resolved decisions — only surfaces what is still unresolved
Loop exits when a reply contains any approval keyword from the manifest
Run state EMAIL_PENDING persists across orchestrator restarts
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/pm-email-types.ts, src/pm-email-loop.ts, src/pm-email-index.ts. Types: DesignBriefInput (issueSummary, affectedModule, relatedIssues, recentPRs, designDocs, issueTitle, issueBody, issueLabels, scoringResult), DesignBrief (issueSummary, affectedModule, relatedOpenIssues, recentPRContext, proposedApproaches, openQuestions), ApproachOption (name, description, pros, cons), FollowUpInput (conversationHistory, latestReply, designBriefInput, resolvedDecisions, unresolvedQuestions), FollowUpResult (responseBody, resolvedDecisions, unresolvedQuestions), PMEmailLoopConfig (pmEmail, replyToAddress, repo, issueNumber, issueTitle, approvalKeywords, runId), PMEmailLoopResult union type (email_sent | reply_processed | approved), DesignBriefGenerator/FollowUpGenerator/EmailStateStore interfaces, PMEmailLoopError. HeuristicBriefGenerator: generates brief with all 6 PRD section 5.2 fields (issue summary, affected module, related open issues, recent PR context, 2-3 proposed approaches with tradeoffs, open questions). Approaches generated from scoring signals (minimal fix always included, coordinated refactor for API/multi-module, comprehensive for many issues). Open questions generated per triggered signal. formatDesignBriefEmail: formats brief into structured email body. HeuristicFollowUpGenerator: analyzes conversation history, extracts decisions, filters resolved questions, generates response with only unresolved items. extractDecisions: recognizes decision patterns (Decision:, let's go with, I prefer, go with, option N, yes/agreed/confirmed). processReply: checks approval keywords first (exits loop on match), generates follow-up otherwise, maintains threading via threadId, persists state. sendDesignBrief: sends initial brief via Gmail MCP, registers thread with watcher, persists state. resumeEmailLoop: loads persisted state and re-registers with watcher for restart-resume. summarizeAgreedDesign: extracts decisions and context from conversation history. createPMReplyHandler: factory for ReplyHandler.onReply that wakes PM agent on reply, routes to onApproval or onReplyProcessed callbacks. Unit tests in src/pm-email-loop.test.ts: 40+ test cases covering HeuristicBriefGenerator (all 6 fields present, issue summary, affected module, related issues formatting, PR context, 2-3 approaches with name/description/pros/cons, open questions from signals, empty issues/PRs handling, comprehensive/refactor approaches), formatDesignBriefEmail (all sections, approval instruction, numbered approaches), extractDecisions (explicit lines, let's go with, I prefer, approach selection, vague replies, yes/agreed), HeuristicFollowUpGenerator (response generation, resolved question removal, new decisions added, never restates resolved, only surfaces unresolved, suggests approval when all resolved), sendDesignBrief (sends email, correct recipient, subject format, brief content, persists state, registers thread, replyTo address), processReply (approval detection, case-insensitive keywords, follow-up on non-approval, persists state, deletes on approval, unregisters thread, appends to history, maintains threadId, no email on approval), resumeEmailLoop (loads state, re-registers, returns null for missing, survives restart), summarizeAgreedDesign (includes decisions, extracts issue summary, turn count), createPMReplyHandler (approval callback, reply callback, throws for unknown run), timing (brief < 5 min, reply < 60s), full conversation flow (multi-turn until approval, maintains threadId).

userStories.title: Issue sweep and scope confirmation
userStories.description: As the user, after I approve a design I want a single email proposing the full set of related open issues this design closes, so I can confirm the scope before a fork is created.
acceptanceCriteria: After approval, PM agent searches all open issues against the agreed design
Produces two groups: high_confidence (design directly closes) and maybe_in_scope (partial overlap), each with one-sentence reasons
Sends scope confirmation email per PRD section 5.3 format with instruction to reply with final issue numbers or 'all'
Parser handles freeform prose replies (e.g. 'include 142 and 156 but drop 201') and 'all'
Confirmed list is passed to the orchestrator as the scope for fork creation
Run state SWEEP_PENDING persists across restarts
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/issue-sweep-types.ts, src/issue-sweep.ts, src/issue-sweep-index.ts. Types: SweepIssue (number, title, labels, reason), SweepResult (highConfidence, maybeInScope), SweepInput (agreedDesign, affectedModule, openIssues, primaryIssueNumber), ScopeConfirmationConfig (pmEmail, replyToAddress, repo, issueNumber, issueTitle, runId), ScopeConfirmationResult union type (scope_email_sent | scope_confirmed), IssueSweeper/SweepStateStore interfaces for testability, SweepError (with phase and runId). HeuristicIssueSweeper: scores issue relevance using title-word overlap with design (0.4 weight), module path matching (0.3 weight), label matching (0.15), and relevance keywords (0.15). Thresholds: >=0.7 high confidence, >=0.4 maybe in scope. Primary issue always included in high confidence. formatScopeEmail: structured email with high_confidence and maybe_in_scope groups, each with one-sentence reasons, instruction to reply with issue numbers or 'all'. parseScopeReply: handles 'all' keyword, explicit includes (142, 156), explicit excludes (drop 201, remove, exclude, skip, except), mixed include/exclude (include 142 and 156 but drop 201), defaults to high confidence on no numbers. sendScopeConfirmation: sends email via Gmail MCP, persists state for restart-resume, registers with watcher. processScopeReply: parses reply, cleans up state, unregisters thread, returns confirmed issue numbers. resumeSweepLoop: loads persisted state and re-registers with watcher for SWEEP_PENDING restart-resume. runIssueSweep: full pipeline (sweep → send email). createSweepReplyHandler: factory for reply callback that processes scope replies and calls onScopeConfirmed. Unit tests in src/issue-sweep.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering HeuristicIssueSweeper (primary issue always high confidence, strong module match, partial overlap categorization, unrelated issues excluded, empty issues, multiple issues varying relevance, label matching), formatScopeEmail (section header, high confidence with numbers/reasons, maybe in scope, instruction text, (none) for empty groups), parseScopeReply ('all' plain/with text/include all, explicit include with numbers, comma-separated, ignores invalid numbers, drop/exclude/remove, mixed include/exclude, no numbers defaults to high confidence, empty reply, prose with numbers), sendScopeConfirmation (sends email, persists state, registers thread, returns sweep result, uses existing thread ID), processScopeReply (parses and returns confirmed numbers, deletes state, unregisters thread), resumeSweepLoop (loads state + re-registers, null for missing, survives restart), runIssueSweep (sweeps and sends, passes thread ID), createSweepReplyHandler (calls onScopeConfirmed, throws for unknown run, cleans up state), SweepError (name, phase, runId).

userStories.title: Build agent for new_feature scaffolding
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need a build agent that scaffolds new modules or features mirroring existing patterns in the repo, so new_feature issues are handled distinctly from bug fixes.
acceptanceCriteria: Receives: agreed design, confirmed issue list, two most similar existing modules (identified by triage), CONTRIBUTING.md or equivalent if present
Mirrors structure of reference modules; does not invent new patterns
Creates module file, test file, and updates any index/registry files the repo uses
Does not update external documentation — defers to a follow-up docs agent pass
Commits with message format: feat({module}): {one-line summary} — closes #{issue_ids}
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/build-agent-types.ts, src/build-agent.ts, src/build-agent-index.ts. Types: BuildAgentInput (designSummary, confirmedIssues, affectedModule, referenceModules, contributingGuide, forkFullName, branchName), BuildAgentResult (success, moduleFiles, testFiles, indexFiles, commitMessage, summary, closesIssues), ReferenceModule (path, files), ScaffoldGenerator/ScaffoldGeneratorOutput interfaces for testability, BuildAgentError (with phase). Functions: formatBuildCommitMessage (feat({module}): {summary} — closes #{ids}), extractModuleName (strips src/lib prefix), verifyForkOnlyAccess (rejects public_repo/repo scopes), validateNoDocumentation (rejects .md/.rst/.txt/.adoc/.mdx), validateModuleScope (ensures files within affected module), validateTestFilePresent (requires test file), validateIndexUpdates (verifies index references new module), analyzeReferenceModules (extracts file patterns, detects tests/index from references). runBuildAgent pipeline: verify token → analyze references → generate scaffold → validate no docs → validate scope → validate tests → validate index → format commit → commit to fork. Receives agreed design, confirmed issues, two reference modules, and CONTRIBUTING.md. Mirrors reference module structure. Creates module file, test file, and index/registry updates. Does not update external documentation (defers to docs agent). Commits with feat({module}): format. Unit tests in src/build-agent.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering formatBuildCommitMessage (single/multiple IDs, src/lib/other prefix), extractModuleName, validateNoDocumentation (.md/.rst/.txt/.adoc/.mdx rejected, source passes, reports all, empty), validateModuleScope (in-module, out-of-scope, trailing slash, empty), validateTestFilePresent (present/absent), validateIndexUpdates (no index, references module, missing reference, full path), analyzeReferenceModules (patterns, tests, index, empty, dedup, __tests__, .spec), verifyForkOnlyAccess (narrow/empty pass, public_repo/repo throw), runBuildAgent integration (success flow, commit message format feat prefix, commits to fork only, rejects upstream access, rejects documentation, rejects out-of-scope, rejects missing tests, empty output, passes input/design/contributing/references to generator, multiple IDs, null contributing guide, mirrors structure, all creates, error phases for doc/scope/test/index validation, no external docs, feat not fix prefix).

userStories.title: Usability agent
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a usability agent that exercises the change as a real user would, so PRs surface developer-experience problems that tests miss.
acceptanceCriteria: Installs the changed package fresh in the sandbox
Exercises the affected API as a new user would (import paths, error messages, common workflows)
Reports on developer experience, not just pass/fail
Output is structured and consumed by the eval agent and PR body
Runs in the same isolated sandbox as the test suite
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/usability-agent-types.ts, src/usability-agent.ts, src/usability-agent-index.ts. Types: UsabilityConfig (forkFullName, branchName, affectedModule, sandboxServices, timeoutMinutes), UsabilityCheck (category, description, status, details, severity), UsabilityCategory union type (import_paths | error_messages | common_workflows | installation | documentation_examples), UsabilityStatus (pass | fail | warning), UsabilitySeverity (critical | major | minor | info), UsabilityAgentInput (forkFullName, branchName, affectedModule, confirmedIssues, sandboxServices, timeoutMinutes, installCommand, entryPoints), UsabilityAgentResult (completed, dxScore, checks, summary, durationSeconds, timedOut, workflowRunUrl, blockers, suggestions), UsabilityExerciser/UsabilityExerciserOutput interfaces for testability, UsabilityAgentError (with phase and forkFullName), USABILITY_WORKFLOW_FILE='usability-test.yml', DEFAULT_USABILITY_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=15. Functions: validateUsabilityConfig (validates forkFullName, branchName, affectedModule, installCommand), buildUsabilityWorkflowInputs (branch, install_command, affected_module, entry_points JSON, timeout, network_policy, sandbox_services), calculateDxScore (weighted deductions: critical=30, major=20, minor=10, info=0; warnings at half weight; floors at 0), extractBlockers (critical+major failures), extractSuggestions (warnings + minor failures), generateUsabilitySummary (markdown report with DX score, install status, check counts, issues found, suggestions), createUsabilityConfig (factory with default timeout, array copy), runUsabilityAgent (full pipeline: validate → trigger workflow_dispatch in same sandbox → poll/wait → exercise API via UsabilityExerciser → score DX → extract blockers/suggestions → return structured output). Installs package fresh via installCommand. Exercises affected API (import paths, error messages, common workflows) via entryPoints. Reports DX score 0-100, not just pass/fail. Output structured for eval agent and PR body (checks array, summary markdown, blockers, suggestions). Runs in same isolated sandbox (same workflow dispatch pattern, network_policy, sandbox_services). Unit tests in src/usability-agent.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering validateUsabilityConfig (valid, empty forkFullName, no slash, empty branch, whitespace branch, empty affectedModule, empty installCommand, phase in error), buildUsabilityWorkflowInputs (branch, install_command, affected_module, entry_points JSON, timeout, network_policy none/allow, sandbox_services join), calculateDxScore (all pass=100, empty=0, critical/major/minor/info deductions, warning half-deduction, accumulation, floor at 0), extractBlockers (critical/major failures, excludes minor/warnings/passing), extractSuggestions (warnings + minor failures, excludes critical/major/passing), generateUsabilitySummary (DX score, install success/failure, check counts, issues found section, suggestions section), createUsabilityConfig (all fields, default timeout, array copy immutability), runUsabilityAgent integration (validates before proceeding, triggers workflow dispatch, polls for run, waits for completion, handles timeout with blockers, exercises API, structured result, DX score 0 on install failure, workflow run URL, duration, dispatch error with phase, exerciser error with phase, DX issues not just pass/fail, same sandbox services/network_policy, structured for eval agent, exercises import paths, handles missing workflow run, configurable timeout), constants (USABILITY_WORKFLOW_FILE, DEFAULT_USABILITY_TIMEOUT_MINUTES).

userStories.title: Regression guard (upstream main diff)
userStories.description: As a maintainer, I want a regression guard that diffs observable behaviour of the fork branch against upstream main, so any behavioural change is flagged before the PR is opened.
acceptanceCriteria: Checks out upstream main in a parallel sandbox job
Runs the same shared test cases on both fork branch and upstream main
Diffs observable outputs and flags any change
Eval agent sets regression_detected=true if any change is found
Regression details appear in the PR body and per-issue verdicts
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/regression-guard-types.ts, src/regression-guard.ts, src/regression-guard-index.ts. Types: RegressionConfig (forkFullName, forkBranchName, upstreamRepo, upstreamDefaultBranch, testCommand, sandboxServices, timeoutMinutes), BranchTestResult (branch, completed, exitCode, stdout, stderr, durationSeconds, timedOut, workflowRunUrl), OutputDiff (category, description, upstream, fork), RegressionResult (regressionDetected, diffs, forkResult, upstreamResult, summary), RegressionGuardError (with phase and branch), REGRESSION_WORKFLOW_FILE='regression-test.yml', DEFAULT_REGRESSION_TIMEOUT_MINUTES=15. Functions: validateRegressionConfig (validates all fields), createRegressionConfig (factory with defaults), buildRegressionWorkflowInputs (test_command, branch, timeout, network_policy, sandbox_services), normalizeOutput (removes timing/timestamps for fair comparison), diffOutputs (compares exit_code, stdout, stderr, timeout status between branches), generateRegressionSummary (markdown for PR body with diff details and workflow links), runRegressionGuard (full pipeline: validate → run tests on fork+upstream in parallel via Promise.all → diff outputs → return regression result). Parallel sandbox jobs via same ActionsClient interface. Same test cases run on both branches (same test_command). Output normalization ignores timing variations. Eval agent integration: regressionDetected=true when diffs found. Regression details structured for PR body (summary) and per-issue verdicts (diffs array). Unit tests in src/regression-guard.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering validateRegressionConfig (valid config, empty/invalid forkFullName, empty branch, invalid upstreamRepo, empty upstreamDefaultBranch, empty testCommand, zero/negative timeout, phase in error), createRegressionConfig (all fields, default timeout, array copy immutability), buildRegressionWorkflowInputs (test_command, branch parameter, timeout, network_policy none/allow, sandbox_services join), normalizeOutput (ms/s/seconds/timestamps replaced, whitespace trimmed, non-timing unchanged, multiple timings), diffOutputs (identical=empty, exit code diff, stdout diff, stderr diff, timeout diff, timing-normalized comparison, multiple diffs, truncation, null vs non-null exit code), generateRegressionSummary (no regressions, with diffs, multiple diffs, workflow links), runRegressionGuard integration (validates before running, triggers both branches in parallel, uses REGRESSION_WORKFLOW_FILE, no regression on identical output, detects exit code/stdout differences, includes both branch results, includes summary, handles timeout, dispatch/run/log errors with phase+branch, correct fork repo, correct test_command, configurable timeout, isolated branch checkouts, regression details for PR body).

userStories.title: Retry loop with eval context injection
userStories.description: As the orchestrator, I need to feed eval retry_context back into the fix or build agent on failure so the agent can correct its own mistakes up to max_retries.
acceptanceCriteria: On overall_pass=false, eval agent emits structured retry_context summarising what failed
Orchestrator re-dispatches the fix or build agent with retry_context appended to its inputs
Retries up to manifest max_retries (default 3)
Retry count is visible in the PR body when the run eventually succeeds
After max_retries exceeded: emails the user with full retry_context from all attempts, labels the upstream issue agent-failed, preserves the fork branch, transitions to FAILED
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/retry-loop-types.ts, src/retry-loop.ts, src/retry-loop-index.ts. Types: RetryAttempt (attemptNumber, retryContext, timestamp, agentType), RetryHistory (runId, maxRetries, attempts), RetryLoopConfig (runId, maxRetries, agentType, upstreamRepo, primaryIssueNumber, pmEmail, replyToAddress, confirmedIssues, forkFullName, branchName), RetryDispatchInput (retryCount, combinedRetryContext, latestRetryContext, agentType), RetryLoopResult union type (retry | max_retries_exceeded), FailureReport (runId, upstreamRepo, primaryIssueNumber, retryHistory, forkBranch, forkFullName, summary), IssueLabeler/FailureNotifier/RetryStateStore interfaces for testability, RetryLoopError (with phase and runId), AGENT_FAILED_LABEL='agent-failed', FAILURE_EMAIL_SUBJECT_PREFIX='[agent-fix] FAILED:'. Functions: buildCombinedRetryContext (structured summary of all previous attempts), recordRetryAttempt (immutable append to history), createRetryHistory (factory), evaluateRetryDecision (retry if attempts < maxRetries, max_retries_exceeded otherwise with failure report), buildFailureSummary (human-readable summary with repo/issues/retries/branch), formatFailureEmail (full markdown email with retry history in code blocks), formatFailureSubject (prefixed subject line), handleMaxRetriesExceeded (best-effort email + label + preserve fork branch), runRetryLoop (full pipeline: load/create history → record attempt → persist → evaluate → handle notifications on terminal → clean up), injectRetryContextForFixAgent (appends combined + latest retry context to design summary), injectRetryContextForBuildAgent (same injection for build agent), resumeRetryLoop (loads persisted state for restart-resume). Eval agent already emits structured retry_context (US-009). Retry count already visible in PR body via buildPRDetails (US-009). Orchestrator transition EVAL_RUNNING→AGENT_RUNNING already exists for retry path (US-002). Unit tests in src/retry-loop.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering createRetryHistory (fields, custom maxRetries), recordRetryAttempt (first attempt, increment, build agent type, immutability, timestamp), buildCombinedRetryContext (empty, all attempts, count header, agent types, timestamps), evaluateRetryDecision (retry when < max, correct retryCount, latest context, combined context, max_retries_exceeded at boundary, failure report fields, maxRetries=0, boundary cases, agent type in dispatch), buildFailureSummary (repo, issues, retry count, agent type, fork branch), formatFailureEmail (header, summary, fork info, all attempts, code blocks, agent types), formatFailureSubject (prefix, repo+issue), handleMaxRetriesExceeded (sends email, labels issue, tolerates email failure, tolerates label failure, success status), runRetryLoop (creates history, loads existing, retry dispatch, max_retries_exceeded trigger, sends email, labels issue, deletes history on terminal, preserves on retry, persists before eval, build agent type), injectRetryContextForFixAgent (appends latest, includes combined, marks latest section, skips empty combined), injectRetryContextForBuildAgent (same behavior), resumeRetryLoop (loads existing, null for missing, survives restart), end-to-end flows (fix retry→retry→exceeded, build context chain, inject produces augmented design), constants (AGENT_FAILED_LABEL, FAILURE_EMAIL_SUBJECT_PREFIX).

userStories.title: Manifest validation, schema versioning, and multi-repo support
userStories.description: As an operator, I need manifest schema versioning and multi-repo support so I can evolve the manifest without breaking existing configured repos and coordinate runs that span multiple repos.
acceptanceCriteria: Manifests declare a schema_version; loader rejects unknown versions with a clear message
Migration path documented for breaking schema changes
Multi-repo manifest support: one logical issue scope can map to coordinated runs across two or more repos
Cross-repo runs share a single email thread and a single eval verdict
Tests cover: old version rejection, multi-repo dispatch, single-thread email aggregation
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/multi-repo-types.ts, src/multi-repo.ts, src/multi-repo-index.ts. Types: SchemaVersion ('1'|'2'), VersionedManifest (extends Manifest with schema_version), MultiRepoManifest (extends VersionedManifest with coordinated_repos), CoordinatedRepo (repo, fork_org?, test_command?, affected_module?), MultiRepoRun (groupRunId, primaryRepo, repos, sharedThreadId, sharedEvalVerdict), RepoRunState (repo, forkFullName, branchName, sandboxPassed?, agentCompleted?), MultiRepoEvalVerdict (overallPass, perRepoVerdicts, combinedSummary), RepoVerdict, SharedEmailThread (groupRunId, threadId, repos, subject), UnsupportedSchemaVersionError (declaredVersion, supportedVersions), MultiRepoCoordinationError (phase, groupRunId), SchemaMigration (from, to, breakingChanges, steps). Constants: SUPPORTED_SCHEMA_VERSIONS=['1','2'], CURRENT_SCHEMA_VERSION='2', SCHEMA_MIGRATIONS with v1→v2 path documenting breaking changes and migration steps. Functions: validateSchemaVersion (returns version or throws UnsupportedSchemaVersionError with clear message including declared+supported versions), validateVersionedManifest (validates schema_version, extracts multi-repo fields, delegates base validation, rejects coordinated_repos on v1), validateCoordinatedRepos (validates array of repos with field-level errors), createMultiRepoRun (creates coordinated run across primary+coordinated repos with deterministic branch names), buildMultiRepoEmailSubject (single subject format for multi-repo thread), createSharedEmailThread (shared thread config for all repos), aggregateMultiRepoEval (single eval verdict: all repos must pass for overallPass=true, combined summary), getMigrationPath (lookup migration descriptor), needsMigration (check if manifest needs upgrade), generateMigrationInstructions (returns steps to migrate). Schema updated: versionedManifestSchema added to src/manifest/schema.ts with schema_version enum and coordinated_repos array schema. Unit tests in src/multi-repo.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering validateSchemaVersion (absent/null→'1', valid versions, rejects unknown '3'/'0'/'latest', error includes declared+supported versions, numeric coercion), validateVersionedManifest (v1 without field, v2 without coordinated_repos, v2 with coordinated_repos, rejects coordinated_repos on v1, rejects unknown version, rejects non-object, validates base fields, applies defaults), validateCoordinatedRepos (valid array, rejects non-array, invalid repo format, non-string fork_org/test_command, non-object entries, empty array, affected_module), createMultiRepoRun (primary only, with coordinated repos, same branch name across repos, sorted issue IDs), buildMultiRepoEmailSubject (single repo format, multi-repo +N format), createSharedEmailThread (multi-repo, single repo), aggregateMultiRepoEval (all pass, any fail, all fail, per-repo verdicts, single repo), getMigrationPath (v1→v2 exists, v2→v3 undefined), needsMigration (absent/v1→true, current→false), generateMigrationInstructions (current→empty, v1→steps), error types (UnsupportedSchemaVersionError fields, MultiRepoCoordinationError fields), constants verification.

userStories.title: Cost guardrails, fork cleanup, and run history dashboard
userStories.description: As an operator, I need a per-run token/cost cap, a fork cleanup policy, and a run history view so the system is operable in production.
acceptanceCriteria: Per-run token and cost cap configurable in the manifest; on breach the run halts and the user is emailed with the partial state
Fork cleanup policy configurable: immediate-after-merge | delayed | never; default delayed-after-merge
Run history dashboard (CLI or minimal web view) lists runs with state, duration, retry count, cost
Test coverage exists across all four scenario types from PRD section 8.1 (simple bug fix, feature with design, retry success, max retries exceeded)
userStories.notes: Implemented in src/cost-guardrails-types.ts, src/cost-guardrails.ts, src/cost-guardrails-index.ts. Types: CostConfig (max_tokens_per_run, max_cost_per_run, cost_per_1k_tokens), RunUsage (runId, tokensUsed, costUsd, lastUpdated), CostCheckResult (withinBudget, breachedCap, usage, config, message), CostBreachNotification (runId, upstreamRepo, primaryIssueNumber, haltedAtState, usage, config, summary), ForkCleanupPolicy ('immediate-after-merge'|'delayed'|'never'), ForkCleanupConfig (policy, delayHours, forkFullName, branchName), ForkCleanupResult (executed, action, scheduledAt, message), ForkCleaner interface, RunHistoryEntry (runId, repo, issueIds, state, durationSeconds, retryCount, costUsd, tokensUsed, createdAt, updatedAt, prUrl), RunHistoryFilter, RunHistoryStats (totalRuns, byState, totalCostUsd, totalTokensUsed, avgDurationSeconds, successRate), RunHistoryStore interface, CostCapBreachedError, ForkCleanupError, ScenarioType union, ScenarioTestResult. Functions: createCostConfig (factory with defaults), checkCostCap (checks token+cost caps), recordAndCheckUsage (accumulates and checks), buildCostBreachNotification (structured notification), formatCostBreachEmail (markdown email), formatCostBreachSubject (prefixed subject), validateForkCleanupPolicy, createForkCleanupConfig (factory with defaults), executeForkCleanup (policy-based: skip/schedule/delete branch/delete fork), executeDelayedCleanup (for scheduled jobs), formatRunHistoryEntry (CLI line), formatDuration (human-readable), formatRunHistoryStats (CLI summary), renderDashboard (full CLI output), queryRunHistory (store query), calculateStats (aggregate from entries), validateScenarioOutcome (PRD 8.1 all 4 scenarios). Constants: DEFAULT_FORK_CLEANUP_POLICY='delayed', DEFAULT_CLEANUP_DELAY_HOURS=72, DEFAULT_COST_PER_1K_TOKENS=0.03, COST_BREACH_EMAIL_SUBJECT_PREFIX. Unit tests in src/cost-guardrails.test.ts: 30+ test cases covering createCostConfig (defaults, overrides, partial), checkCostCap (no caps within budget, under token cap, token breach, under cost cap, cost breach, token checked first, includes usage/config), recordAndCheckUsage (records and checks, accumulates, detects breach after accumulation, calculates cost), buildCostBreachNotification (token breach, cost breach, state in summary), formatCostBreachEmail (all sections), formatCostBreachSubject (prefix), CostCapBreachedError (token/cost fields), validateForkCleanupPolicy (valid/invalid), createForkCleanupConfig (defaults, custom), executeForkCleanup (never skips, delayed schedules, immediate deletes branch, deletes fork when empty, throws ForkCleanupError), executeDelayedCleanup (executes immediate), ForkCleanupError (fields), formatDuration (s/m/h), formatRunHistoryEntry (complete/zero cost), calculateStats (empty, multiple entries, no completed runs), formatRunHistoryStats (all fields), renderDashboard (empty/with entries), queryRunHistory (calls store), validateScenarioOutcome (simple_bug_fix pass/fail states, feature_with_design pass/fail, retry_success pass/fail, max_retries_exceeded pass/fail, unknown scenario), constants verification.
